testCases.name: test_agent_performance[test_data2]
testCases.input: Find the name of the customer who ordered a 'Wireless Mouse'
testCases.actualOutput: SELECT c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE p.name = 'Wireless Mouse'
retrievalContext: Expected Logic: Join customers, orders, order_items, and products to filter by product name
Expected SQL: SELECT c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE p.name = 'Wireless Mouse'

metricsData.name: SQL Semantic Correctness [GEval]
metricsData.reason: The actual output SQL query is semantically identical to the expected logic and directly addresses the user's input question. The retrieval context also provided the exact expected logic and SQL, indicating strong alignment.
